Here the heavy arguments being used in France and
England by opponents of the female suffrage movement
= oman is more crafty and cunning than man, natur-
ally, and being given the power would be less
honest"


must admit that the two master works of humanity
are her monopoly to create the child and comfort the man.
She can't do better than stick to them.''


TO admit the first proposition is to admit that woman is
more bralny than She would be more successfully
dishonest We dont know how it is abroad but if woman
In America could be dishonest than man has been or
make worse mess of government politics than man has,
1t surely would be due to superior natural capacity


AS to the second propositions Does stick those
TWO so-caed master works soley Not in this country


There hardly young in this whole nation but is
lnfluenced, one way another for good or bad by
woman There few married who do not work and
vote without regard to the welfare desires of the
WIFE, and the exceptions to this rule mainly who
have no adequate conception of their political obligations
and hence their enfranchsement is farce The question:
IN this country is not to whether womens influence
shall be confined to breeding and the comorng of man.
She has already got far beyond that because men realize
that her influence in all things is good But the question
here is as to whether her influence shall be positive
direct and have full definite force The question is not
Is she equal; but, should she have equality?


Maybe they tie her up the cradle and cookstove
over there but over here she broke those chains long
which is one OF the why this country bit ahead
of any other on the whole map
